WHITE COAT FORGE

Learning Goals & Progression

10 learning goals. 5-point scale. This is how you get through.

How It Works

The 10 learning goals articulate what trainees need to be, do, and know. Each is assessed on a five-point scale across the three training phases. Trainees must meet phase-end standards to progress or complete the program.

Be — Competencies Scale

Level Standard
L1Needs to work on behaviour in more than 5 domains of professional practice
L2Needs to work on behaviour in 4 or 5 domains of professional practice
L3Needs to work on behaviour in 2 or 3 domains of professional practice
L4Needs to work on behaviour in 1 domain of professional practice
L5Consistently behaves in line with all 10 domains of professional practice

Do — EPA Scale

Level Standard
L1Is able to be present and observe
L2Is able to act with direct supervision
L3Is able to act with indirect supervision (ready access to a supervisor)
L4Is able to act with supervision at a distance (limited access to a supervisor)
L5Is able to supervise others

Know — Knowledge Guide Scale

Level Standard
L1Has heard of some of the topics in this knowledge guide
L2Knows the topics and concepts in this knowledge guide
L3Knows how to apply this knowledge to practice
L4Frequently shows they apply this knowledge to practice
L5Consistently demonstrates application of this knowledge to practice

Phase Progression

Foundation → Consolidation

Meet phase-end progression criteria across Be, Do, and Know learning goals.

Consolidation → Completion

Meet phase-end progression criteria. Written Examination awaits early in Completion.

Completion criteria

Pass Written + Clinical Examinations. Meet all completion-level standards across all 10 learning goals.

White Coat Forge — RACP Basic Training Reference  ·  Not affiliated with RACP
